self_improvement.claim: Directive Workspace can present queue lifecycle state more truthfully when routed entries are explicitly downgraded once canonical truth shows the live case already progressed or broke.
self_improvement.mechanism: This DEEP bounded result adds one routed-status guard that derives `routed_progressed` and `routed_inconsistent` from canonical current-head truth.
self_improvement.baseline_observation: Before this slice, many queue entries still surfaced raw routed status even when their live current head had already advanced to bounded result, promotion-readiness, implementation-result, or a broken route anchor.
self_improvement.expected_effect: The queue surface will stop overstating active routing while preserving raw routed status as stored queue evidence and leaving still-live routed stubs unchanged.

decision.rationale: This bounded routed-status slice closes the main remaining queue lifecycle mismatch seam without broadening into queue sync or generic stale-status repair.
decision.runtime_threshold_check: yes - the mechanism is still valuable without a runtime surface, so Architecture should retain product-owned value
